content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To         :    File No. T-90-353                          Date  :    May 16, 1990


From  :    FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
           Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel

Subject :  Telephone Advice Provided to Justice David Eagleson, California
           Supreme Court


     On May 15 and 16, 1990, I provided telephone advice to
Supreme Court Justice David Eagleson about his ability to accept a
$3,500 honorarium from McGeorge School of Law for giving the
commencement address on May 19, 1990.

     I advised Justice Eagleson that Government Code Section 85400
prohibits any elected state or local officer from accepting an
honorarium in excess of $1,000 per calendar year from a single
source in connection with a speech on the governmental process.
(See also Regulation 18540.) As a person who holds elective
office, as defined in Government Code Section 82023, he is subject
to this prohibition.

     I reviewed a draft copy of Justice Eagleson's speech and
determined that it generally concerns how to be a good lawyer, a
subject which is not related to the governmental process. I
expressed concern, however, that on page 17, his speech refers to
the attorney disciplinary responsibilities of the California Supreme
Court, a subject that would appear to relate to the governmental
process. He said he would eliminate the sentence which refers to
the California Supreme Court and instead say, "Scores of attorneys
are disbarred and disciplined every year." I advised him that,
with that change, his speech did not relate to the governmental
process and that the honoraria limit in Section 85400 would
therefore not apply. A copy of his draft speech is attached.

     We also discussed whether acceptance of an honorarium in
excess of $1,000 would raise questions from the press about his
taking advantage of a legal technicality. We agreed that issue
involves a personal choice on his part, rather than a legal duty.

cc:   Chairman John H. Larson

FPPC

May 21 10:31 AM '90

Supreme Court of California

STATE BUILDING
350 McALLISTER STREET
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94102

DAVID N. EAGLESON
ASSOCIATE JUSTICE

May 17, 1990


Ms. Kathy Donovan
General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices
Commission
428 J Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     Thank you for counseling with me with respect to the
speech I intend to deliver at the McGeorge Law School gradua-
tion ceremony on Saturday, May 19.

     I have deleted any possible reference to govern-
mental activities which, in the stretch in someone's imagina-
tion, might be construed to be in violation of Prop. 73.

     Thank you for your counsel and my best wishes for a
speedy and easy delivery of your first child.

                                        Respectfully yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        DAVID N. EAGLESON
DNE/cr
